---
title: "VM Installation Guide"
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

## VM Installation Guide

This is the clean-install checklist for a future MS project-management VM.

## 1. Base Packages

```bash
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y git curl rsync apache2 apache2-utils openssl sqlite3 \
  build-essential libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev libxml2-dev r-base r-base-dev \
  default-jdk
```

Run the repository checker after cloning:

```bash
cd /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management
bash scripts/check_requirements.sh
bash scripts/check_requirements.sh --install
bash scripts/check_requirements.sh --install-r
```

The R package list includes `shiny`, `shinyjs`, `DBI`, `RSQLite`, `digest`, `DT`, `rJava`, and `mailR`.

## 2. GitHub Access

```bash
ssh -T git@github.com
```

If access fails, add the VM public key as a deploy key in the GitHub repository:

```bash
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```

Clone the repository:

```bash
cd /home/<user>
git clone git@github.com:yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management.git
```

## 3. Shiny Server

```bash
sudo apt-get install -y gdebi-core
wget https://download3.rstudio.org/ubuntu-20.04/x86_64/shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb
sudo gdebi shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb
sudo systemctl enable shiny-server
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
```

Create the app folder:

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo usermod -a -G shiny "$USER"
```

## 4. Deploy App Files

```bash
cd /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management
sudo rsync -av --delete --exclude 'ms_projects.db' --exclude '.Renviron' ms-app/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
```

## 5. Initialize Database

```bash
cd /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo -u shiny Rscript setup_database.R
```

For schema/seed checks without deleting the DB:

```bash
sudo -u shiny MS_RESET_DB=0 Rscript setup_database.R
```

## 6. Runtime Environment

```bash
sudo tee /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron >/dev/null <<'EOF'
AUTH_MODE=ldap
TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
MS_FACILITY_EMAIL=ms-service@biochem.mpg.de
MS_MAIL_FROM=ms-service@biochem.mpg.de
MS_UPLOAD_ROOT=/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs-ms-projects
MS_MAX_UPLOAD_MB=75
EOF
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo chmod 640 /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

## 7. Apache / LDAP

Install LDAP client tooling:

```bash
sudo apt-get install -y ldap-utils libldap2-dev libsasl2-dev ca-certificates
```

Configure OpenLDAP trust:

```bash
sudo tee /etc/ldap/ldap.conf >/dev/null <<'EOF'
BASE            dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
URI             ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de
TLS_REQCERT     hard
TLS_CACERTDIR   /etc/ssl/certs
EOF
```

Copy and enable the vhost:

```bash
sudo cp /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management/scripts/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel headers rewrite ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite ms-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2
```

## 8. Verification

```bash
curl -Ik https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
curl -Ik -u USER https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/ms-app/
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3838/ms-app/
```

Local maintenance login:

```bash
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=local/' /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

Return to LDAP:

```bash
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=ldap/' /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

## 9. Backup / Restore

Backup:

```bash
sudo rsync -av /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)/
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db.bak.$(date +%F-%H%M%S)
```

Restore:

```bash
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo rsync -av --delete /srv/shiny-server/ms-app.backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMM/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
```
